evals.prompt: I have a SaaS landing page at example.com/product. We sell a project management tool, have 3 pricing tiers ($0, $29, $99), and about 800 reviews on G2 with 4.6 stars. Add schema markup to this page.
evals.expected_output: Should produce SoftwareApplication schema with AggregateOffer (3 tiers), AggregateRating, Organization with @id reference, BreadcrumbList. Should include knowsAbout and sameAs recommendations. Should mention Speakable if there's quotable content.

evals.prompt: Here's our existing schema, it has errors: {"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Article","author":"John Doe","datePublished":"January 5, 2025","headline":"How to optimize your website"}. Fix it and make it better for AI visibility.
evals.expected_output: Should identify 3 errors: author as plain string (not Person object), date not in ISO 8601, missing required properties (image, publisher). Should fix all issues, add @id references, add speakable, add mainEntityOfPage, recommend adding author sameAs and knowsAbout.

evals.prompt: I run a biotech company that makes molecular biology reagents. We have a homepage, about 50 product pages, a blog with research articles, and an FAQ section. What schema should we implement across the site and in what order?
evals.expected_output: Should provide a prioritized implementation plan: 1) Organization+WebSite on homepage with knowsAbout for molecular biology topics, 2) BreadcrumbList sitewide, 3) Product schema on product pages, 4) Article/BlogPosting with author Person entities on blog, 5) FAQPage on FAQ section, 6) Speakable on key articles, 7) sameAs expansion. Should recommend @id strategy for cross-page entity linking.
